messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "colo-weights-enterprise-slice-misallocation",
 "title": "Colo-MW geography weights are wrong by construction for the 29% enterprise slice — the Netherlands cell is partly phantom",
 "narrative": "Every geo share was apportioned on Statista colocation MW, but the segment axis includes 29% enterprise on-premise demand — and enterprise machine rooms are distributed by industrial base, not by where Amsterdam colo campuses stand. EUDCA's own framing says colo+hyperscale is only about two-thirds of Europe's IT power; the missing third has a different map. NL is the most colo-concentrated market in the set (~70% of its capacity in Amsterdam metro), while Switzerland is self-operation-heavy (installed-base method gives 0.13 vs the curated 0.08) and Germany's installed load exceeds its colo figure by ~1,000MW. Multiplying colo-weighted geography by an enterprise segment share manufactures enterprise PDU demand in the Netherlands that physically sits in German and Swiss machine rooms. Anyone sizing a DACH enterprise channel off these weights will staff the wrong countries.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "geo.NL",
  "geo.CH",
  "seg.enterprise"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Commercial colocation + hyperscale provide only ~two-thirds of Europe's total IT power — roughly a third of real rack-PDU-consuming load (enterprise on-prem) is invisible to the colo-MW denominator the geo shares were built on",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c11",
    "ledger:geo.NL"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The non-colo third is not distributed like the colo two-thirds: NL capacity is colo-hub concentrated, while CH's installed-base cross-check already runs to 0.13 vs the curated 0.08 and DE's installed load exceeds colo by ~1,000MW; the funnel nonetheless multiplies the same colo-derived geo vector against seg.enterprise = 0.29",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.CH",
    "ledger:geo.DE",
    "ledger:seg.enterprise"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "For ~29% of segment scope the geo weights systematically overstate NL and understate CH (and DE at the installed-load scope) — geo.NL = 0.28 counts an enterprise slice that is not there, so the NL×enterprise cell is partly phantom and the CH×enterprise cell is undersized",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.NL",
    "ledger:geo.CH",
    "ledger:seg.enterprise"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "Both axes are individually well-sourced — Statista for geography, Synergy for segment — so each ledger row passes review on its own. The error exists only in the product of the two axes; no single node carries it, no band covers it, and the ledger's colo-vs-installed cross-checks read as per-country noise rather than a systematic per-segment misallocation.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "A consistent country-level installed-IT-load table (e.g., EUDCA country profiles) showing NL's share of CE-7 total installed load ≈ its colo share (~0.28) — i.e., the non-colo third distributes the same way the colo two-thirds does.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.5,
  "rationale": "The colo-vs-installed divergence is already documented in the ledger's own cross-checks for DE, CH, AT and PL (all diverging in the direction this hypothesis predicts); only the magnitude and the NL side are unconfirmed.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.NL",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.23,
   "note": "NL weight corrected for its near-zero enterprise on-prem contribution to the 29% slice"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.CH",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.11,
   "note": "Mix-shift: self-operated Swiss load reallocated in — a reallocation, not a shrink; the risk is misdirected go-to-market, not lost TAM"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Publication of installed-load vs colocation country splits for CE-7",
   "where": "EUDCA 'State of European Data Centres' country profiles; BMWK/Bitkom German installed-load updates",
   "threshold": "DE/CH installed-load shares diverging >5pp from their colo shares",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Enterprise-segment pipeline geography vs model weights in early selling",
   "where": "Venture's own CRM: country origin of enterprise/mid-operator inbound and distributor leads",
   "threshold": "Enterprise leads from CH+DE running >2x their model-weighted share vs NL",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission or assemble a single-scope installed-IT-load table across CE-7 (the ledger's own promotion path) to re-derive geo weights per segment rather than globally",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "geo.CH",
   "note": "One consistent scope resolves NL/CH/DE simultaneously"
  },
  {
   "action": "Split the v2 model's geography axis by segment: colo-weighted geo for hyperscale/colo demand, installed-load-weighted geo for enterprise — and weight the enterprise channel build toward DE/CH industrial accounts",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Removes the independence assumption where it is demonstrably false"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "structure-independence",
 "riskId": "risk.structure-independence.colo-weights-enterprise-slice-misallocation"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

EUDCA's own framing says colo+hyperscale is only about two-thirds of Europe's IT power; the missing third has a different map. NL is the most colo-concentrated market in the set (~70% of its capacity in Amsterdam metro), while Switzerland is self-operation-heavy (installed-base method gives 0.13 vs the curated 0.08) and Germany's installed load exceeds its colo figure by ~1,000MW. the risk is misdirected go-to-market, not lost TAM"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Publication of installed-load vs colocation country splits for CE-7","where":"EUDCA 'State of European Data Centres' country profiles; BMWK/Bitkom German installed-load updates","threshold":"DE/CH installed-load shares diverging >5pp from their colo shares","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Enterprise-segment pipeline geography vs model weights in early selling","where":"Venture's own CRM: country origin of enterprise/mid-operator inbound and distributor leads","threshold":"Enterprise leads from CH+DE running >2x their model-weighted share vs NL","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission or assemble a single-scope installed-IT-load table across CE-7 (the ledger's own promotion path) to re-derive geo weights per segment rather than globally","type":"information","voiNodeId":"geo.CH","note":"One consistent scope resolves NL/CH/DE simultaneously"},{"action":"Split the v2 model's geography axis by segment: colo-weighted geo for hyperscale/colo demand, installed-load-weighted geo for enterprise — and weight the enterprise channel build toward DE/CH industrial accounts","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Removes the independence assumption where it is demonstrably false"}]}
